mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity repricing within the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment rises during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer and business demand, which compresses corporate earnings forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline fails to predict negative returns with a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 5-year out-of-sample window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of stable inflation reflects robust manufacturing capacity utilization, which supports near-term earnings revisions and generates positive equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes statistically insignificant when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.5% annually.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds the 12-month moving average of core inflation by more than 1.5 percentage points, real borrowing costs become restrictive, suppressing economic activity and producing negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 60% of threshold-crossing events or the real rate differential shows zero predictive IC.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the consumer base and supports wage-driven demand, which translates into higher corporate revenue projections and positive S&P 500 performance over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC drops below 0.01 or payroll growth fails to correlate with quarterly equity returns during structural labor shifts.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where quarterly GDP growth decelerates while retail sales accelerate indicates inventory buildup and weakening final demand, which forces earnings downgrades and triggers negative equity returns within one month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth falls while RSAFS growth rises
failure_condition: The divergence pattern yields positive returns in more than half of observed cases or loses statistical significance after controlling for monetary policy shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with falling unemployment during economic recoveries signals improving capacity utilization and labor absorption, which drives immediate positive equity repricing over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in over 50% of recovery-phase instances or fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark with p < 0.05.
